title: OOF hazard tables instead of fixed tyre-life heuristics
summary: This target is already a discrete next-lap event, so treat each row as a hazard state and learn empirical pit hazard curves rather than hard-coding expected life constants.
rationale: This target is already a discrete next-lap event, so treat each row as a hazard state and learn empirical pit hazard curves rather than hard-coding expected life constants. Discrete-time survival literature fits this setup directly, and F1 strategy sources emphasize that stop timing depends on stint phase and degradation, not just absolute tyre age.
implementation_hint: Build smoothed out-of-fold hazard features such as P(pit next lap | Compound, TyreLife bin, Stint, RaceProgress bin, dry/wet) and logit-transform them. Compute them only on training folds grouped by `Race_Year`, then feed them to the existing tree model.
expected_effect: Should outperform fixed compound windows by learning nonlinear pit windows and late-race effects directly from data; likely modest but real CV gain if leakage is controlled.
risk: Naive target encoding will leak badly. Rare bins need smoothing/backoff to coarser groupings.
